mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, labor market slack signals severe demand destruction and rising recession probabilities, causing equity risk premiums to spike and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, which expands corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive mean return over 60 days in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power when controlled for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a rising index of industrial production reflects capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, which directly translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism yields negative or flat 20-day returns during recovery phases, or the correlation between INDPRO growth and SPX returns drops below 0.10 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index signals accelerating inflation that forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 65% of months with rising CPIAUCSL, or the signal's Rank IC remains negative across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 2.0 percent quarterly, robust aggregate demand supports corporate earnings revisions, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 55% of quarters meeting the threshold, or earnings revision data shows no concurrent positive shift.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than half of observed instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate environment, any upward revision in the federal funds rate triggers a repricing of duration risk, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as growth expectations are discounted.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of rate hike instances within this regime, or the negative correlation breaks down during liquidity-driven market rallies.
